title: Return to Work Bonus Eligibility
description: What are the conditions and timeline for claiming a return-to-work bonus after starting a new job, particularly for someone with a CDI who has been on RMI/RSA?
narrative: The user is seeking clarification on whether they are eligible to claim a return-to-work bonus after one month of employment, despite being told otherwise by a government agency. They have recently started a CDI job after a period of unemployment supported by RMI (Revenu de Solidarité Active). The user believes they meet the criteria based on legislation, especially since the bonus was reportedly available for individuals with a DDA (Contrat à Durée Déterminée) after just one month of work. The user is concerned about the discrepancy between their understanding of the law and the agency's refusal, possibly due to fraud concerns. Relevant documents confirm that the bonus existed until 2010 and could be paid after one month under certain conditions, though it was replaced by the RSA. The user’s intent is to verify their legal right to the bonus and understand the correct procedure for claiming it, particularly in light of conflicting information from official sources.
